revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 富山中教院 |画像 = [[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|200px]] |所在地 = [[富山県]][[富山市]]中央通り2丁目4 | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 41 | 緯度秒 = 21 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 137 |経度分 = 13 | 経度秒 = 13.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Toyama |祭神 = |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''富山中教院'''（とやまちゅうきょういん）とは、[[富山県]][[富山市]][[中央通り (富山市)|中央通り]]にある[[神社]]である。中教院の名は明治初期に各県において設置された[[大教院]]の下部組織である中教院に由来し、当時は広大な敷地を有していたが、[[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）現在は幅2メートルの小さな祠となっている。また中教院の前の通りは「中教院前通り」と呼ばれる商店街となっている『中教院（富山）の夜店始まる』[[北日本新聞]] [[1977年]]7月2日[[朝刊]]16面。 == 背景 == [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）3月13日、明治政府は[[太政官 (明治時代)|太政官]]布達により「此度王政復古神武創業ノ始ニ被為基諸事御一新祭政一致之御制度ニ御回復被遊候ニ付テハ先第一神祇官御再興御造立ノ土追追初祭奠モ可被為興儀被仰出候」として神道を基として古代の遺風を恢復する[[祭政一致]]の方針を明らかにし内閣記録局編、『法規分類大全第一編 社寺門一 神社一』（1頁）、1891年（明治24年）5月、内閣記録局、次いで3月28日には「中古以来其権現或ハ牛頭天王之類其外仏語ヲ以神号ニ相称候神社不少候何レモ其神社之由緒委細ニ書付早早可申出候事（中略）仏像ヲ以神体ト致候神社ハ以来相改可申候事」として[[神仏分離]]の政策を打出した内閣記録局編、『法規分類大全第一編 社寺門一 神社一』（1及び2頁）、1891年（明治24年）5月、内閣記録局。この神仏分離の政策は民間において[[廃仏毀釈]]の運動となって現れ、同年6月22日には[[東本願寺]]等を通じてそれが政府の意図と異なることが宣布されたにもかかわらず、各地において廃仏毀釈は実行されていった矢吹慶輝、『日本精神と日本仏教』（123及び124頁）、1935年（昭和10年）1月、仏教聯合会。しかし、このような神道国教化の政策は各所において行詰りを見せ、より現実的な施策をとるべきだとの意見が強まったので、明治政府は[[1871年]]（明治4年）8月8日に[[神祇官]]を[[神祇省]]に改組し、[[1872年]]（明治5年）3月14日には神祇省を廃止して[[教部省]]を設置した富山県編、『富山県史 通史編 近代上』（1066頁）、1981年（昭和56年）3月、富山県。このような政策の転換に乗じて、仏教各宗派は連名を以て同年5月13日に「神道ヲ始メ釈漢洋諸科学ヨリ宇内各国ノ政治風俗農功物産ニ至ルマテ悉ク之ヲ講習シ海外ノ講師ニ愧サラシメ人材ヲ棟育シ頑固迂僻ノ悪習ヲ一洗」する神仏混合の皇道宣布機関である[[大教院]]の設立を建白し、5月31日には裁可を受けて、6月1日に設立された小川原正道、「大教院の制度と初期の活動」、『武蔵野短期大学研究紀要』第16巻（125頁より136頁）、2002年（平成14年）6月、武蔵野短期大学。この大教院の下部組織として各[[都道府県庁所在地|府県庁所在地]]に設置されたのが、中教院だったのである富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981年（昭和56年）3月、富山県。 == 沿革 == [[File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|thumb|明治時代の富山中教院]] [[新川県]]においては[[1873年]]（明治6年）10月29日に富山餌指町宣教館跡地に中教院が落成し、11月6日から10日にかけて祭典を行った富山県編、『富山県史 年表』（225頁）、1987年（昭和62年）3月、富山県。当時の新川県[[県令|権令]][[山田秀典]]は県内の[[浄土真宗]]各寺院が求めていた合議所設置の件に対して次のような達を与え、落成した中教院への協力を呼びかけている富山県編、『富山県史 史料編Ⅵ 近代上』（1422頁）、1978年（昭和53年）10月、富山県。 {{quotation|今般県下富山元宣教舘跡ヘ中教院設立之儀神道七宗教導職等遂合議既ニ落成ニ及ヒ来月六日弥開講可相成筈之処此程真宗局ゟ合議所取設之儀申来候由伝聞いたし候当県下之儀ハ前条之通リ最早中教院落成ニも相成居候ニ付仮令真宗局ゟ其寺院等ヘ右様申来居候とも右ニ相泥ミ中教院之儀差措候次第ハ無之筈ニ候間兼而布達および置候通御趣旨ヲ奉シ其宗末寺一同ヘ申諭シ仝院ヘ参会倶ニ尽力可有之}}この中教院が所在した場所はもとの[[勝興寺]]支坊境内であり、それが大きく削られて中教院の敷地とされたことに上述のような神仏分離、あるいは廃仏毀釈の思潮の影響を見る説もある富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999年（平成11年）9月、富山市郷土博物館。富山中教院においても主導権を握っていたのは神官であったというが、一方において元来浄土真宗の影響が強い富山においては実際の布教活動は僧侶が行うことが多かったという側面もあり、全国的にも僧侶と神官の間における対立は次第に深まっていった富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069及び1070頁）、1981年（昭和56年）3月、富山県。このような経緯により浄土真宗各派は盛んに大教院分離運動を展開して、[[1875年]]（明治8年）2月に大教院を離脱し、同年4月30日には太政官より神仏合同布教を禁ずる旨の通達があって、大教院は当初の目的を完全に失することとなった。これによって大教院は同年5月3日に解散するに至ったのである。 大教院の解散によって従前の富山中教院もまたその機能を失うこととなったが、その敷地内には[[1875年]]（明治8年）3月28日に設置された[[神道事務局]]の分局である中教院が新たに置かれており、これが[[1882年]]（明治15年）からは教派神道の一つである[[神宮教]]（後の神宮奉斎会）の経営に係る富山分教会所となって、[[伊勢神宮]][[祭主]][[久邇宮朝彦親王]]の允許を得てその神霊を祀った富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939年（昭和14年）1月、富山県。この際には[[伯爵]][[冷泉為紀]]が直々に神霊を奉持しており、稲垣[[上新川郡]]郡長は県境に赴いてこれを奉迎し、富山は全町を挙げて日章旗を掲げこれを迎えたという。[[1885年]]（明治18年）5月31日の富山大火により社殿は一時焼亡し、神霊は一時[[於保多神社]]に奉遷されたが、同年11月には社殿等を再建した富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1070及び1071頁）、1981年（昭和56年）3月、富山県。[[1899年]]（明治32年）9月には神宮奉斎会の設立に伴い、名称を神宮奉斎会富山支部と変更している。 その後、神宮奉斎会富山支部は[[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）に富山市鹿島町の[[富山縣護國神社]]近辺に移転し、大神宮と名称を変更しているが、[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）8月2日の富山大空襲において全焼し消滅した富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51年）8月、富山新聞社。神宮奉斎会富山支部が移転したのは、1928年（昭和3年）10月21日に開業した[[富山地方鉄道富山軌道線|富山市営軌道]]東部線[[西町停留場|西町]] - 東田地方間を開通させるにあたり、その土地が必要であったからであるという富山地方鉄道株式会社編、『富山地方鉄道五十年史』（870頁）、1983年（昭和58年）3月、富山地方鉄道。富山市営軌道東部線開業と同時に神宮教会所跡地には通坊前[[路面電車停留場|停留場]]が開業したが、[[1959年]]（昭和34年）9月11日に移転した際に中教院前停留場と名称を変更している今尾恵介監修、『日本鉄道旅行地図帳 全線・全駅・全廃線 6号』（36頁）、2008年（平成20年）10月、新潮社高瀬重雄編、『日本歴史地名大系第16巻 富山県の地名』（450頁）、2001年（平成13年）7月、平凡社。この中教院前停留場は[[1961年]]（昭和36年）7月18日に富山地方鉄道富山軌道線山室線が開業すると同時に、同線の停留場ともなったが、[[1972年]]（昭和47年）9月21日に東部線中教院前 - [[地鉄ビル前停留場|地鉄ビル前]]間が廃止され、続いて山室線も[[1984年]]（昭和59年）3月31日に廃線となったのに伴って姿を消した富山地方鉄道株式会社編、『富山地方鉄道五十年史』（894及び896頁）、1983年（昭和58年）3月、富山地方鉄道富山地方鉄道編、 『富山地方鉄道70年史 この20年のあゆみ』 （97頁）、2000年（平成12年）、富山地方鉄道。しかし、その後も[[バス停留所]]名として中教院前の名は残存している。 このように中教院が神道事務局ないし富山分教会所と名を変えていき、あるいは移転した後にもその名は地域に根付いており富山大百科事典編集事務局編、『富山大百科事典 下巻』（240頁）、1994年（平成6年）8月、北日本新聞社、[[1904年]]（明治37年）頃からは夏季にその境内前に夜店が立ち並ぶようになって中教院の夜店として親しまれるようになった。この近辺には[[1885年]]（明治18年）5月31日の富山大火の反省によって計画され、[[1887年]]（明治20年）6月11日に竣工式を挙げた防火水路が通っており、その沿線に植えられた柳や松の並木は夏の夜の納涼に多くの人々を集めた富山市役所編、『富山市史』（375及び389頁）、1909年（明治42年）9月、富山市役所。この中教院前の夜店は戦時下において一時絶えていたが、[[1946年]]（昭和21年）7月1日に復活し、人々をよろこばせたという富山市史編纂委員会編、『富山市史 第三巻』（50頁）、1960年（昭和35年）4月、富山市史編纂委員会。昭和40年代は7月初頭から8月初旬まで、昭和50年代には7月末まで、夏の風物詩として中教院前通りと、交差する[[中央通り (富山市)|中央通り商店街]]の一部で盛大に行われていたが『あつ〜い土曜日 ゴルフで"汗" 夜店で"涼"』北日本新聞 [[1982年]]7月11日朝刊20面、両商店街の衰退もあり徐々に期間が短くなり、1990年代初頭には中断された『マイトーク 富山市中央通り商栄会事業部イベント委員長 ○○○さん』北日本新聞 [[1998年]]7月30日朝刊22面。 戦後になって中教院の由緒を知る人々は、再び富山中教院が所在していた場所に[[祠]]を築いた。この祠の幅は約2メートル程であり、日本一小さい神社であるとも言われている[http://webun.jp/item/7330611 （117）中教院前バス停（ちゅうきょういんまえ 富山市）] - 2016年（平成28年）12月5日、北日本新聞社[http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html 商店街の娘 - BBTスペシャル] - 2008年（平成20年）5月26日、富山テレビ放送。 社殿の老朽化に伴い、2020年に改修が行われた{{cite news|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/?fbclid=IwAR1_nLSQiPKdIgPoVJRAa3T62UL7RPd0iS_1Qh3WVJhZGXL8TlI88gupU58|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」　氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|publisher=富山経済新聞|date=2020-11-27|accessdate=2020-11-28}}。 [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[University]], [[Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[World War II]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[University]], [[Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[World War II]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[University]], [[Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[World War II]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-chozuya 1.jpg|Chozuya 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-chozuya 2.jpg|Chozuya 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-closeup.jpg|Close-up of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-full view from street.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin from the Street File:Toyama Chukyoin-full.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden altar closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden Altar File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Taikyo Institute]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Toyama]] [[Category:1873 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Toyama Chukyoin]] }} [[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:oyama Chukyoin]] [[ja:富山中教院]] [[en:Toyama Chukyoin]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Shrines in Toyama, Toyama
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[University]], [[Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[World War II]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[University]], [[Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[World War II]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto [[sn:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:oyama Chukyoin]] [[ja:富山中教院]] {{Commons category|Toyama Chukyoin}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[World War II]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-full view from street.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin from the Street File:Toyama Chukyoin-full.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden altar closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden Altar File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Taikyo Institute]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:1873 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:oyama Chukyoin]] [[ja:富山中教院]] {{Commons category|Toyama Chukyoin}}

It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[World War II]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-full view from street.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin from the Street File:Toyama Chukyoin-full.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden altar closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden Altar File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Taikyo Institute]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:1873 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:oyama Chukyoin]] [[ja:富山中教院]] {{Commons category|Toyama Chukyoin}}

Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30929140]] Wikidata name: Toyama Chukyoin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-chozuya 1.jpg|Chozuya 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-chozuya 2.jpg|Chozuya 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-closeup.jpg|Close-up of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-full view from street.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin from the Street File:Toyama Chukyoin-full.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden altar closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden Altar File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Taikyo Institute]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:1873 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:oyama Chukyoin]] [[ja:富山中教院]] {{Commons category|Toyama Chukyoin}} {{wikidata link|Q30929140}}

It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30929140]] Wikidata name: Toyama Chukyoin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30929140]] Wikidata name: Toyama Chukyoin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama
