Chukyoin-full view from street.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin from the Street File:Toyama Chukyoin-full.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden altar closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden Altar File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. [[sn:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30929140]] Wikidata name: Toyama Chukyoin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30929140]] Wikidata name: Toyama Chukyoin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-full view from street.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin from the Street File:Toyama Chukyoin-full.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden altar closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden Altar File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Taikyo Institute]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:1873 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[ja:富山中教院]] {{Commons category|Toyama Chukyoin}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30929140]] Wikidata name: Toyama Chukyoin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-full view from street.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin from the Street File:Toyama Chukyoin-full.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden altar closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden Altar File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Taikyo Institute]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:1873 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q30929140}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30929140]] Wikidata name: Toyama Chukyoin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} {{translated page|ja|富山中教院|version=101455472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[ja:富山中教院]] {{Commons category|Toyama Chukyoin}} {{wikidata link|Q30929140}} [[Category:1873 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[co:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30929140]] Wikidata name: Toyama Chukyoin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a [[:en:Prefectures of Japan|Prefecture of Japan]].. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]].富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for [[:en:University|University]], [[:en:Middle school|Middle school]], and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-full view from street.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin from the Street File:Toyama Chukyoin-full.jpg|Full View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden altar closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden Altar File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber and curtain.jpg|Interior of the Honden and Curtain File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden chamber.jpg|Interior of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden closeup.jpg|Close-up of the Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-honden.jpg|Honden File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji over stele.jpg|Omikuji on top of a Stele File:Toyama Chukyoin-omikuji tied.jpg|Tied Omikuji File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-1.jpg|Panorama View 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-2.jpg|Panorama View 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-3.jpg|Panorama View 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-4.jpg|Panorama View 4 File:Toyama Chukyoin-panorama-5.jpg|Panorama View 5 File:Toyama Chukyoin-plaque.jpg|Plaque of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-sign.jpg|Signboard of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-stele.jpg|Stele of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-street view.jpg|Street View of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of Honden with Torii Shimenawa at the top.jpg|View of the Honden with Shimenawa on the Torii File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of the city looking out of the shrine with chozuya.jpg|View of the City from the Shrine with Chozuya File:Toyama Chukyoin-view of torii and interior.jpg|View of the Torii and Interior File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * [[:en:Toyama Gokoku Shrine|Toyama Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Taikyo It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a {{ill|Prefectures of Japan|en|Prefectures of Japan|WD=Q50337|lt=Prefecture of Japan}}.. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}}.富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for {{ill|University|en|University|WD=Q3918|lt=University}}, {{ill|Middle school|en|Middle school|WD=Q149566|lt=Middle school}}, and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near {{ill|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|en|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q11456842|lt=Toyama Gokoku Shrine}} to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}} the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[co:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[sn:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in {{ill|Toyama (city)|en|Toyama (city)|WD=Q204266|lt=Toyama City}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a {{ill|Prefectures of Japan|en|Prefectures of Japan|WD=Q50337|lt=Prefecture of Japan}}.. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}}.富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for {{ill|University|en|University|WD=Q3918|lt=University}}, {{ill|Middle school|en|Middle school|WD=Q149566|lt=Middle school}}, and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near {{ill|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|en|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q11456842|lt=Toyama Gokoku Shrine}} to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}} the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near {{ill|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|en|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q11456842|lt=Toyama Gokoku Shrine|1=Toyama Gokoku Shrine}} to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. [[co:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[co:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in {{ill|Toyama (city)|en|Toyama (city)|WD=Q204266|lt=Toyama City|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a {{ill|Prefectures of Japan|en|Prefectures of Japan|WD=Q50337|lt=Prefecture of Japan|1=Prefectures of Japan}}.. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}.富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for {{ill|University|en|University|WD=Q3918|lt=University|1=University}}, {{ill|Middle school|en|Middle school|WD=Q149566|lt=Middle school|1=Middle school}}, and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near {{ill|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|en|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q11456842|lt=Toyama Gokoku Shrine|1=Toyama Gokoku Shrine}} to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[co:commons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Toyama Chukyoin]][[ja:富山中教院]][[simple:Toyama Chukyoin]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in {{ill|Toyama (city)|en|Toyama (city)|WD=Q204266|lt=Toyama City|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a {{ill|Prefectures of Japan|en|Prefectures of Japan|WD=Q50337|lt=Prefecture of Japan|1=Prefectures of Japan}}.. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}.富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for {{ill|University|en|University|WD=Q3918|lt=University|1=University}}, {{ill|Middle school|en|Middle school|WD=Q149566|lt=Middle school|1=Middle school}}, and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near {{ill|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|en|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q11456842|lt=Toyama Gokoku Shrine|1=Toyama Gokoku Shrine}} to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. See name on the right 富山中教院]] == Gallery == File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-1.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 1 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-2.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 2 File:Toyama Chukyoin Omikuji vending machine-3.jpg|Omikuji Vending Machine 3 File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|Exterior of Chukyoin File:Toyama Chukyoin-banner 2.jpg|Chukyoin Banner 2 File:Toyama File:Toyama Chukyoin-view slightly inside.jpg|View Slightly Inside File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|Photo of Chukyoin ==Related pages== * {{ill|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|en|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q11456842|lt=Toyama Gokoku Shrine|1=Toyama Gokoku Shrine}} * [[Taikyo Institute]] * [[Yohashira Shrine]] formerly known as Nagano Chukyoin ==Sources== {{reflist}} {{state shinto}} Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near {{ill|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|en|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q11456842|lt=Toyama Gokoku Shrine|1=Toyama Gokoku Shrine}} to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyama Chukyoin|religious_affiliation=[[Jōdo Shinshū]] (until 1873)
[[Shinto]] (1873-present)
[[Taikyo Institute]] (1873-1875)
[[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]] (1875-1882)
[[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] (1882-1945)
[[Association of Shinto Shrines]] (1945-present)| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Toyama Chukyoin(1).png|caption=Toyama Chukyoin in the Meiji period|coordinates=36.689151424824914, 137.22031614938365|deity={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}} {{ill|Amaterasu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}|consecration_year=1873|qq=draft}} {{ill|Toyama Chukyoin|ja|富山中教院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Chukyoin|qq=}} (富山中教院) is a small shrine in {{ill|Toyama (city)|en|Toyama (city)|WD=Q204266|lt=Toyama City|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web|last=Natsuko|first=Kawanishi|date=1999-09-17|orig-date=1999-09-17|title=博物館だより|trans-title=Museum news|url=https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230407013502/https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm|archive-date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-09|website=www.city.toyama.toyama.jp}}https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/file_upload/100767/_main/100767_03.pdf The shrine's grounds were originally a Buddhist temple of the [[Jōdo Shinshū]] sect, but it was later converted into a Chukyoin in 1873. Chukyoin was a type of institution created to promote the teachings of the [[Taikyo Institute]]. There was one on each capital of a {{ill|Prefectures of Japan|en|Prefectures of Japan|WD=Q50337|lt=Prefecture of Japan|1=Prefectures of Japan}}.. The [[Taikyo Institute]], literally {{Nihongo|'''Great Teaching Institute'''|大教院|Taikyoin}} had sub institutions across Japan called {{Nihongo|Medium Teaching Institute|中教院|Chukyoin}} and {{Nihongo|Small Teaching Institute|小教院|Shokyoin}}. Every prefecture had its own Chukyoin in its capital and this was the one of {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}.富山県編、『富山県史 通史編Ⅴ 近代上』（1069頁）、1981st year （昭和56年）3月、富山県 Shokyoins were scattered all across the country. The size comparison resembles the words for {{ill|University|en|University|WD=Q3918|lt=University|1=University}}, {{ill|Middle school|en|Middle school|WD=Q149566|lt=Middle school|1=Middle school}}, and [[Primary school|Elementary school]]. All of them however promoted the same [[:en:Taikyo proclamation|Taikyo proclamation]] teachings. In 1875 the Taikyo Institute was closed, and the building became an office of the [[Bureau of Shinto Affairs]]. Then in 1882 it became a [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]] office.富山市郷土博物館編、『[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/muse/tayori/tayori33/tayori33.htm 博物館だより第33号]』、1999th year （平成11th year ）9月、富山市郷土博物館富山県編、『富山県政史 第七巻』（629頁より631頁)、1939th year （昭和14th year ）1月、富山県 Around the Chukyoin site, there was a night market that became famous in 1904. In 1925, the Chukyoin was relocated near {{ill|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|en|Toyama Gokoku Shrine|WD=Q11456842|lt=Toyama Gokoku Shrine|1=Toyama Gokoku Shrine}} to make way for a new streetcar line. Only a small shrine remains at the original location, as the streetcar was later removed. The Chukyoin (now affiliated with [[:en:Jingukyo|Jingukyo]]) was completely destroyed on August 2, 1945 during an air raid.富山新聞社大百科事典編集部編、『富山県大百科事典』（540及び541頁）、1976年（昭和51st year ）8月、富山新聞社 During {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} the night market was damaged and unused until its repair in 1950. It was still operational in 1999. and 2011{{Cite web|last=mika4413|title=中教院前の夜店|url=http://blog.livedoor.jp/mika4413/archives/52185396.html|access-date=2023-04-10|website=富山のミカエル日記|language=ja}} After the war people rebuilt the shrine on its original location. The shrine can be found next to the bus stop Chukyoin Mae. It is known as the smallest shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|date=2010-06-16|title=Bbtスペシャル｜最新情報|url=http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|access-date=2023-04-09|archive-date=2010-06-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100616073619/http://www.bbt.co.jp/bspe/index337.html|url-status=bot: unknown}} The shrine building was rennovated in 2020.{{Cite web|title=富山中教院で「正遷座祭」 氏子たちによる御神体の移動も|url=https://toyama.keizai.biz/headline/1159/|access-date=2023-04-09|website=富山経済新聞}}[[File:Toyama Chukyoin.jpg|thumb|Remnants of Toyama Chukyoin today.
